CGAL::Poisson_mesh_domain_3.name: CGAL::Poisson_mesh_domain_3
CGAL::Poisson_mesh_domain_3.brief: The class Poisson_mesh_domain_3 derives from Labeled_mesh_domain_3 for the handling of Poisson_reconstruction_function .
CGAL::Poisson_mesh_domain_3.detailed: This class has a constructor taking a labeling function. It has also a static template member function that acts as named constructor: create_Poisson_mesh_domain() , to create a domain from a Poisson_reconstruction_function BGT is a geometric traits class that provides the basic operations to implement intersection tests and intersection computations through a bisection method. This parameter must be instantiated with a model of the concept BisectionGeometricTraits_3 . Is model of MeshDomain_3 CGAL::Labeled_mesh_domain_3 CGAL::make_mesh_3()

members.name: Poisson_mesh_domain_3
members.brief: Construction from a function, a bounding object and a relative error bound.
members.detailed: Bounding_object either a bounding sphere (of type Sphere_3 ), a bounding box (type Bbox_3 ), or a bounding Iso_cuboid_3 NamedParameters a sequence of Named Parameters function the Poisson reconstruction function bounding_object the bounding object bounding the meshable space. np an optional sequence of Named Parameters among the ones listed below: Optional Named Parameters <button class="collapsible"> relative_error_bound </button> the relative error bound used to compute intersection points between the implicit surface and query segments. The bisection is stopped when the length of the intersected segment is less than the product of relative_error_bound by the diameter of the bounding object. Default: FT (1e-3)

members.name: Poisson_mesh_domain_3
members.brief: Construction from a function, a bounding object and a relative error bound.
members.detailed: NamedParameters a sequence of Named Parameters function the Poisson reconstruction function np an optional sequence of Named Parameters among the ones listed below: Optional Named Parameters <button class="collapsible"> relative_error_bound </button> the relative error bound used to compute intersection points between the implicit surface and query segments. The bisection is stopped when the length of the intersected segment is less than the product of relative_error_bound by the diameter of the bounding object. Default: FT (1e-3)

members.name: create_Poisson_mesh_domain
members.brief: Construction from a Poisson implicit function.
members.detailed: This static method is a named constructor . It constructs a domain whose bounding surface is described implicitly as the zero level set of a function. The domain to be discretized is assumed to be the domain where the function has negative values. NamedParameters a sequence of Named Parameters Bounding_object either a bounding sphere (of type Sphere_3 ), a bounding box (type Bbox_3 ), or a bounding Iso_cuboid_3 which is required to circumscribe the surface and to have its center inside the domain. function the Poisson reconstruction function bounding_object object bounding the meshable domain and its center is inside the domain. np an optional sequence of Named Parameters among the ones listed below: Optional Named Parameters <button class="collapsible"> relative_error_bound </button> is the relative error bound, relative to the diameter of the box of the image. Default: FT (1e-3)
members.section: Creation of domains from Poisson implicit functions

CGAL::Poisson_reconstruction_function.name: CGAL::Poisson_reconstruction_function
CGAL::Poisson_reconstruction_function.brief: Implementation of the Poisson Surface Reconstruction method.
CGAL::Poisson_reconstruction_function.detailed: Given a set of 3D points with oriented normals sampled on the boundary of a 3D solid, the Poisson Surface Reconstruction method [2] solves for an approximate indicator function of the inferred solid, whose gradient best matches the input normals. The output scalar function, represented in an adaptive octree, is then iso-contoured using an adaptive marching cubes. Poisson_reconstruction_function implements a variant of this algorithm which solves for a piecewise linear function on a 3D Delaunay triangulation instead of an adaptive octree. Gt Geometric traits class. Is model of ImplicitFunction

members.name: Poisson_reconstruction_function
members.brief: Creates a Poisson implicit function from the range of points [first, beyond) .
members.detailed: InputIterator iterator over input points. PointPMap is a model of ReadablePropertyMap with a value_type = Point . It can be omitted if InputIterator value_type is convertible to Point . NormalPMap is a model of ReadablePropertyMap with a value_type = Vector .
members.argsstring: (InputIterator first, InputIterator beyond, PointPMap point_pmap, NormalPMap normal_pmap)

params.name: first
params.brief: iterator over the first input point.

params.name: beyond
params.brief: past-the-end iterator over the input points.

params.name: point_pmap
params.brief: property map: value_type of InputIterator -> Point (the position of an input point).

params.name: normal_pmap
params.brief: property map: value_type of InputIterator -> Vector (the oriented normal of an input point).

members.name: bounding_sphere
members.brief: Returns a sphere bounding the inferred surface.

members.name: compute_implicit_function
members.brief: This function must be called after the insertion of oriented points.
members.detailed: It computes the piecewise linear scalar function operator() by: applying Delaunay refinement, solving for operator() at each vertex of the triangulation with a sparse linear solver, and shifting and orienting operator() such that it is 0 at all input points and negative inside the inferred surface. SparseLinearAlgebraTraits_d Symmetric definite positive sparse linear solver. If Eigen 3.1 (or greater) is available and CGAL_EIGEN3_ENABLED is defined, an overload with Eigen_solver_traits<Eigen::ConjugateGradient<Eigen_sparse_symmetric_matrix<double>::EigenType> > as default solver is provided. solver sparse linear solver. smoother_hole_filling controls if the Delaunay refinement is done for the input points, or for an approximation of the surface obtained from a first pass of the algorithm on a sample of the points. false if the linear solver fails.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: ImplicitFunction interface: evaluates the implicit function at a given 3D query point.
members.detailed: The function compute_implicit_function() must be called before the first call to operator() .

members.brief: Returns a point located inside the inferred surface.
